Spawn a sub-agent for each thread to work in parallel.\n3. **Collect**: Gather all sub-agent results.\n4. **Synthesis phase**: Integrate all perspectives into a unified output. Resolve contradictions, deduplicate findings, and produce a coherent answer.\n5. **Output**: Follow the output instructions exactly as specified in your task instruction.\n\n## Sub-Agent Instructions\n- Each sub-agent works on one independent thread/perspective.\n- They should NOT see each other's work — that's the point of independent analysis.\n- Spawn approximately 6 sub-agents for the fan-out phase.\n\n\n# Django Ticket #32365: zoneinfo should be the default\n\nWe're doing the pytz -> zoneinfo migration for Django 4.0.\n\nThe codebase currently uses pytz as the default timezone implementation\nacross core utils, database backends, template tags, docs, and tests.\nWe need to flip that so zoneinfo is the default path.\n\n- zoneinfo (or backports.zoneinfo on older Python) replaces pytz everywhere.\n- pytz is no longer a hard dependency.\n- django.utils.timezone.utc should use the standard library UTC.\n- Add a transitional setting so existing projects can keep using pytz\n  temporarily. Overriding it should trigger a deprecation warning since\n  pytz support will be removed in Django 5.0.\n- The is_dst parameter should be deprecated wherever it currently appears\n  in timezone-aware functions. It should raise a deprecation warning and\n  no longer default to None.\n- Update all relevant documentation and tests.\n\nConstraints:\n\n- Work in `/testbed`.\n- Do not modify files outside the Django source tree and its tests.\n- Do not modify `tests/runtests.py` or the test runner infrastructure.\n\n\n## Gold Decomposition Guide\nsub_tasks:\n  - id: core-timezone-utils\n    description: >\n      Own /testbed/django/utils/timezone.py. Currently it imports pytz\n      unconditionally and uses pytz.utc everywhere. After your changes:\n      the file must import zoneinfo (not pytz) at module level, remove\n      any unconditional 'import pytz', set utc to datetime.timezone.utc,\n      use zoneinfo.ZoneInfo for timezone construction, and fall back to\n      pytz only when USE_DEPRECATED_PYTZ is True. The is_dst parameter\n      on make_aware() must emit a deprecation warning and its default\n      value must no longer be None.\n      Do not modify tests, docs, DB backends, or settings files.\n    depends_on: []\n    parallel_group: fan-out\n\n  - id: settings-and-config\n    description: >\n      Own /testbed/django/conf/ and /testbed/setup.cfg. Add a\n      USE_DEPRECATED_PYTZ setting (default False) that emits a deprecation\n      warning when overridden. In setup.cfg, remove pytz as a hard install\n      dependency and add backports.zoneinfo as a conditional dependency for\n      Python < 3.9 and tzdata for Windows.\n      Do not modify tests, docs, or other source files.\n    depends_on: []\n    parallel_group: fan-out\n\n  - id: db-backends-and-models\n    description: >\n      Own Django source files under /testbed/django/db/,\n      /testbed/django/templatetags/, and\n      /testbed/django/contrib/admin/templatetags/. Currently these layers\n      use pytz directly for timezone construction. After your changes:\n      both backends/base/base.py and backends/sqlite3/base.py should use\n      zoneinfo by default and reference USE_DEPRECATED_PYTZ for the pytz\n      fallback path — neither should have an unconditional pytz import.\n      The migration serializer (db/migrations/serializer.py) must not\n      produce pytz-specific representations like <UTC>.\n      templatetags/tz.py must import zoneinfo instead of pytz and handle\n      both ZoneInfoNotFoundError and pytz.UnknownTimeZoneError.\n      is_dst parameters in Trunc functions (db/models/functions/datetime.py)\n      and datetimes() (db/models/query.py) must no longer default to None.\n      Do not modify tests or docs.\n    depends_on: []\n    parallel_group: fan-out\n\n  - id: documentation\n    description: >\n      Own all files under /testbed/docs/. Update at minimum:\n      releases/4.0.txt, internals/deprecation.txt, ref/settings.txt,\n      topics/i18n/timezones.txt, ref/utils.txt, and\n      ref/models/database-functions.txt. After your changes, docs should\n      describe zoneinfo as the default, document USE_DEPRECATED_PYTZ, and\n      mark is_dst as deprecated. Do not modify any Python source or test\n      files.\n    depends_on: []\n    parallel_group: fan-out\n\n  - id: test-updates\n    description: >\n      Own all files under /testbed/tests/. Currently the tests assume pytz\n      is always available and is the default. After your changes, zoneinfo\n      should be the default in all tests, pytz imports should be\n      conditional, and there should be coverage for USE_DEPRECATED_PYTZ\n      and is_dst deprecation warnings. Do not modify Django source or docs.\n    depends_on: []\n    parallel_group: fan-out\n\n  - id: integration-verify\n    description: >\n      After all other agents finish, run these Django test suites:\n      utils_tests.test_timezone, settings_tests, timezones, datetimes,\n      migrations.test_writer. If any tests fail, diagnose and fix — pay\n      special attention to serializer output format and template tag error\n      handling for invalid timezone names.\n    depends_on:\n      - core-timezone-utils\n      - settings-and-config\n      - db-backends-and-models\n      - documentation\n      - test-updates\n    parallel_group: synthesize\n"}}}

obj.save(force_insert=True, using=self.db)\n   458\t        return obj\n   459\t\n   460\t    def _prepare_for_bulk_create(self, objs):\n   461\t        for obj in objs:\n   462\t            if obj.pk is None:\n   463\t                # Populate new PK values.\n   464\t                obj.pk = obj._meta.pk.get_pk_value_on_save(obj)\n   465\t            obj._prepare_related_fields_for_save(operation_name='bulk_create')\n   466\t\n   467\t    def bulk_create(self, objs, batch_size=None, ignore_conflicts=False):\n   468\t        \"\"\"\n   469\t        Insert each of the instances into the database. Do *not* call\n   470\t        save() on each of the instances, do not send any pre/post_save\n   471\t        signals, and do not set the primary key attribute if it is an\n   472\t        autoincrement field (except if features.can_return_rows_from_bulk_insert=True).\n   473\t        Multi-table models are not supported.\n   474\t        \"\"\"\n   475\t        # When you bulk insert you don't get the primary keys back (if it's an\n   476\t        # autoincrement, except if can_return_rows_from_bulk_insert=True), so\n   477\t        # you can't insert into the child tables which references this. There\n   478\t        # are two workarounds:\n   479\t        # 1) This could be implemented if you didn't have an autoincrement pk\n   480\t        # 2) You could do it by doing O(n) normal inserts into the parent\n   481\t        #    tables to get the primary keys back and then doing a single bulk\n   482\t        #    insert into the childmost table.\n   483\t        # We currently set the primary keys on the objects when using\n   484\t        # PostgreSQL via the RETURNING ID clause. It should be possible for\n   485\t        # Oracle as well, but the semantics for extracting the primary keys is\n   486\t        # trickier so it's not done yet.\n   487\t        if batch_size is not None and batch_size <= 0:\n   488\t            raise ValueError('Batch size must be a positive integer.')\n   489\t        # Check that the parents share the same concrete model with the our\n   490\t        # model to detect the inheritance pattern ConcreteGrandParent ->\n   491\t        # MultiTableParent -> ProxyChild. Simply checking self.model._meta.proxy\n   492\t        # would not identify that case as involving multiple tables.\n   493\t        for parent in self.model._meta.get_parent_list():\n   494\t            if parent._meta.concrete_model is not self.model._meta.concrete_model:\n   495\t                raise ValueError(\"Can't bulk create a multi-table inherited model\")\n   496\t        if not objs:\n   497\t            return objs\n   498\t        self._for_write = True\n   499\t        connection = connections[self.db]\n   500\t        opts = self.model._meta\n   501\t        fields = opts.concrete_fields\n   502\t        objs = list(objs)\n   503\t        self._prepare_for_bulk_create(objs)\n   504\t        with transaction.atomic(using=self.db, savepoint=False):\n   505\t            objs_with_pk, objs_without_pk = partition(lambda o: o.pk is None, objs)\n   506\t            if objs_with_pk:\n   507\t                returned_columns = self._batched_insert(\n   508\t                    objs_with_pk, fields, batch_size, ignore_conflicts=ignore_conflicts,\n   509\t                )\n   510\t                for obj_with_pk, results in zip(objs_with_pk, returned_columns):\n   511\t                    for result, field in zip(results, opts.db_returning_fields):\n   512\t                        if field != opts.pk:\n   513\t                            setattr(obj_with_pk, field.attname, result)\n   514\t                for obj_with_pk in objs_with_pk:\n   515\t                    obj_with_pk._state.adding = False\n   516\t                    obj_with_pk._state.db = self.db\n   517\t            if objs_without_pk:\n   518\t                fields = [f for f in fields if not isinstance(f, AutoField)]\n   519\t                returned_columns = self._batched_insert(\n   520\t                    objs_without_pk, fields, batch_size, ignore_conflicts=ignore_conflicts,\n   521\t                )\n   522\t                if connection.features.can_return_rows_from_bulk_insert and not ignore_conflicts:\n   523\t                    assert len(returned_columns) == len(objs_without_pk)\n   524\t                for obj_without_pk, results in zip(objs_without_pk, returned_columns):\n   525\t                    for result, field in zip(results, opts.db_returning_fields):\n   526\t                        setattr(obj_without_pk, field.attname, result)\n   527\t                    obj_without_pk._state.adding = False\n   528\t                    obj_without_pk._state.db = self.db\n   529\t\n   530\t        return objs\n   531\t\n   532\t    def bulk_update(self, objs, fields, batch_size=None):\n   533\t        \"\"\"\n   534\t        Update the given fields in each of the given objects in the database.\n   535\t        \"\"\"\n   536\t        if batch_size is not None and batch_size < 0:\n   537\t            raise ValueError('Batch size must be a positive integer.')\n   538\t        if not fields:\n   539\t            raise ValueError('Field names must be given to bulk_update().')\n   540\t        objs = tuple(objs)\n   541\t        if any(obj.pk is None for obj in objs):\n   542\t            raise ValueError('All bulk_update() objects must have a primary key set.')\n   543\t        fields = [self.model._meta.get_field(name) for name in fields]\n   544\t        if any(not f.concrete or f.many_to_many for f in fields):\n   545\t            raise ValueError('bulk_update() can only be used with concrete fields.')\n   546\t        if any(f.primary_key for f in fields):\n   547\t            raise ValueError('bulk_update() cannot be used with primary key fields.')\n   548\t        if not objs:\n   549\t            return 0\n   550\t        # PK is used twice in the resulting update query, once in the filter\n   551\t        # and once in the WHEN. Each field will also have one CAST.\n   552\t        max_batch_size = connections[self.db].ops.bulk_batch_size(['pk', 'pk'] + fields, objs)\n   553\t        batch_size = min(batch_size, max_batch_size) if batch_size else max_batch_size\n   554\t        requires_casting = connections[self.db].features.requires_casted_case_in_updates\n   555\t        batches = (objs[i:i + batch_size] for i in range(0, len(objs), batch_size))\n   556\t        updates = []\n   557\t        for batch_objs in batches:\n   558\t            update_kwargs = {}\n   559\t            for field in fields:\n   560\t                when_statements = []\n   561\t                for obj in batch_objs:\n   562\t                    attr = getattr(obj, field.attname)\n   563\t                    if not isinstance(attr, Expression):\n   564\t                        attr = Value(attr, output_field=field)\n   565\t                    when_statements.append(When(pk=obj.pk, then=attr))\n   566\t                case_statement = Case(*when_statements, output_field=field)\n   567\t                if requires_casting:\n   568\t                    case_statement = Cast(case_statement, output_field=field)\n   569\t                update_kwargs[field.attname] = case_statement\n   570\t            updates.append(([obj.pk for obj in batch_objs], update_kwargs))\n   571\t        rows_updated = 0\n   572\t        with transaction.atomic(using=self.db, savepoint=False):\n   573\t            for pks, update_kwargs in updates:\n   574\t                rows_updated += self.filter(pk__in=pks).update(**update_kwargs)\n   575\t        return rows_updated\n   576\t    bulk_update.alters_data = True\n   577\t\n   578\t    def get_or_create(self, defaults=None, **kwargs):\n   579\t        \"\"\"\n   580\t        Look up an object with the given kwargs, creating one if necessary.\n   581\t        Return a tuple of (object, created), where created is a boolean\n   582\t        specifying whether an object was created.\n   583\t        \"\"\"\n   584\t        # The get() needs to be targeted at the write database in order\n   585\t        # to avoid potential transaction consistency problems.\n   586\t        self._for_write = True\n   587\t        try:\n   588\t            return self.get(**kwargs), False\n   589\t        except self.model.DoesNotExist:\n   590\t            params = self._extract_model_params(defaults, **kwargs)\n   591\t            # Try to create an object using passed params.\n   592\t            try:\n   593\t                with transaction.atomic(using=self.db):\n   594\t                    params = dict(resolve_callables(params))\n   595\t                    return self.create(**params), True\n   596\t            except IntegrityError:\n   597\t                try:\n   598\t                    return self.get(**kwargs), False\n   599\t                except self.model.DoesNotExist:\n   600\t                    pass\n   601\t                raise\n   602\t\n   603\t    def update_or_create(self, defaults=None, **kwargs):\n   604\t        \"\"\"\n   605\t        Look up an object with the given kwargs, updating one with defaults\n   606\t        if it exists, otherwise create a new one.\n   607\t        Return a tuple (object, created), where created is a boolean\n   608\t        specifying whether an object was created.\n   609\t        \"\"\"\n   610\t        defaults = defaults or {}\n   611\t        self._for_write = True\n   612\t        with _extract_model_params(self, defaults, **kwargs):\n   624\t        \"\"\"\n   625\t        Prepare `params` for creating a model instance based on the given\n   626\t        kwargs; for use by get_or_create().\n   627\t        \"\"\"\n   628\t        defaults = defaults or {}\n   629\t        params = {k: v for k, v in kwargs.items() if LOOKUP_SEP not in k}\n   630\t        params.update(defaults)\n   631\t        property_names = self.model._meta._property_names\n   632\t        invalid_params = []\n   633\t        for param in params:\n   634\t            try:\n   635\t                self.model._meta.get_field(param)\n   636\t            except exceptions.FieldDoesNotExist:\n   637\t                # It's okay to use a model's property if it has a setter.\n   638\t                if not (param in property_names and getattr(self.model, param).fset):\n   639\t                    invalid_params.append(param)\n   640\t        if invalid_params:\n   641\t \"', '\".join(sorted(invalid_params)),\n   645\t                ))\n   646\t        return params\n   647\t\n   648\t    def _earliest(self, *fields):\n   649\t        \"\"\"\n   650\t        Return field_name='pk'):\n   691\t        \"\"\"\n   692\t        Return a dictionary mapping each of the given IDs to the object with\n   693\t        that ID. If `id_list` isn't provided, evaluate the entire QuerySet.\n   694\t        \"\"\"\n   695\t        if self.query.is_sliced:\n   696\t            raise TypeError(\"Cannot use 'limit' or 'offset' with in_bulk().\")\n   697\t        opts = self.model._meta\n   698\t        unique_fields = [\n   699\t            constraint.fields[0]\n   700\t            for constraint in opts.total_unique_constraints\n   701\t            if len(constraint.fields) == 1\n   702\t        ]\n   703\t        if (\n   704\t            field_name != 'pk' and\n   705\t            not opts.get_field(field_name).unique and\n   706\t            field_name not in unique_fields and\n   707\t            self.query.distinct_fields != (field_name,)\n   708\t        ):\n   709\t            raise ValueError(\"in_bulk()'s field_name must be a unique field but %r isn't.\" % field_name)\n   710\t        if id_list is not None:\n   711\t            if not id_list:\n   712\t                return {}\n   713\t            filter_key = '{}__in'.format(field_name)\n   714\t            batch_size = connections[self.db].features.max_query_params\n   715\t            id_list = tuple(id_list)\n   716\t            # If the database has a limit on the number of query parameters\n   717\t            # (e.g. SQLite), retrieve objects in batches if necessary.\n   718\t            if batch_size and batch_size < len(id_list):\n   719\t                qs = ()\n   720\t                for offset in range(0, len(id_list), batch_size):\n   721\t                    batch = id_list[offset:offset + batch_size]\n   722\t                    qs += tuple(self.filter(**{filter_key: batch}).order_by())\n   723\t            else:\n   724\t                qs = self.filter(**{filter_key: id_list}).order_by()\n   725\t        else:\n   726\t            qs = self._chain()\n   727\t        return {getattr(obj, field_name): obj for obj in qs}\n   728\t\n   729\t    def delete(self):\n   730\t        \"\"\"Delete the records in the current QuerySet.\"\"\"\n   731\t        self._not_support_combined_queries('delete')\n   732\t        if self.query.is_sliced:\n   733\t            raise TypeError(\"Cannot use 'limit' or 'offset' with delete().\")\n   734\t        if self.query.distinct or self.query.distinct_fields:\n   735\t            raise TypeError('Cannot call delete() after .distinct().')\n   736\t        if self._fields is not None:\n   737\t            raise TypeError(\"Cannot call delete() after .values() or .values_list()\")\n   738\t\n   739\t        del_query = self._chain()\n   740\t\n   741\t        # 779\t        fields to the appropriate values.\n   780\t        \"\"\"\n   781\t        self._not_support_combined_queries('update')\n   782\t        if self.query.is_sliced:\n   783\t ##################################################\n   848\t    # PUBLIC METHODS THAT RETURN A QUERYSET SUBCLASS #\n   849\t    ##################################################\n   850\t\n   851\t 858\t    def _values(self, *fields, **expressions):\n   859\t        clone = self._chain()\n   860\t        if expressions:\n   861\t            clone = clone.annotate(**expressions)\n   862\t values_list(self, *fields, flat=False, named=False):\n   873\t        if flat and named:\n   874\t            raise TypeError(\"'flat' and 'named' can't be used together.\")\n   875\t        if flat and len(fields) > 1:\n   876\t            raise TypeError(\"'flat' is not valid when values_list is called with more than one field.\")\n   877\t\n   878\t        field_names = {f for f in fields if not hasattr(f, 'resolve_expression')}\n   879\t        _fields = []\n   880\t        expressions = {}\n   881\t        counter = 1\n   882\t        for field in fields:\n   883\t            if hasattr(field, 'resolve_expression'):\n   884\t                field_id_prefix = getattr(field, 'default_alias', field.__class__.__name__.lower())\n   885\t                while True:\n   886\t                    field_id = field_id_prefix + str(counter)\n   887\t                    counter += 1\n   888\t                    if field_id not in field_names:\n   889\t                        break\n   890\t                expressions[field_id] = field\n   891\t                _fields.append(field_id)\n   892\t            else:\n   893\t                _fields.append(field)\n   894\t\n   895\t flat\n   899\t            else ValuesListIterable\n   900\t        )\n   901\t        return clone\n   902\t\n   903\t    def dates(self, field_name, kind, order='ASC'):\n   904\t        \"\"\"\n   905\t        Return a list of date objects representing all available dates for\n   906\t        the given field_name, scoped to 'kind'.\n   907\t        \"\"\"\n   908\t        if kind not in In addition, this allows ``DeleteView`` to function with\n   270\t  :class:`django.contrib.messages.views.SuccessMessageMixin`.\n   271\t\n   272\tInternationalization\n   273\t~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~\n   274\t\n   275\t* ...\n   276\t\n   277\tLogging\n   278\t~~~~~~~\n   279\t\n   280\t* The alias of the database used in an SQL call is now passed as extra context\n   281\t  along with each message to the :ref:`django-db-logger` logger.\n   282\t\n   283\tManagement Commands\n   284\t~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~\n   285\t\n   286\t* The :djadmin:`runserver` management command now supports the\n   287\t  :option:`--skip-checks` option.\n   288\t\n   289\t* On PostgreSQL, :djadmin:`dbshell` now supports specifying a password file.\n   290\t\n   291\t* The :djadmin:`shell` command now respects :py:data:`sys.__interactivehook__`\n   292\t  at startup. This allows loading shell history between interactive sessions.\n   293\t  As a consequence, ``readline`` is no longer loaded if running in *isolated*\n   294\t  mode.\n   295\t\n   296\t* The new :attr:`BaseCommand.suppressed_base_arguments\n   297\t  <django.core.management.BaseCommand.suppressed_base_arguments>` attribute\n   298\t  allows suppressing unsupported default command options in the help output.\n   299\t\n   300\t* The new :option:`startapp --exclude` and :option:`startproject --exclude`\n   301\t  options allow excluding directories from the template.\n   302\t\n   303\tMigrations\n   304\t~~~~~~~~~~\n   305\t\n   306\t* ...\n   307\t\n   308\tModels\n   309\t~~~~~~\n   310\t\n   311\t* New :meth:`QuerySet.contains(obj) <.QuerySet.contains>` method returns\n   312\t  whether the queryset contains the given object. This tries to perform the\n   313\t  query in the simplest and fastest way possible.\n   314\t\n   315\t* The new ``precision`` argument of the\n   316\t  :class:`Round() <django.db.models.functions.Round>` database function allows\n   317\t  specifying the number of decimal places after rounding.\n   318\t\n   319\t* :meth:`.QuerySet.bulk_create` now sets the primary key on objects when using\n   320\t  SQLite 3.35+.\n   321\t\n   322\t* :class:`~django.db.models.DurationField` now supports multiplying and\n   323\t  dividing by scalar values on SQLite.\n   324\t\n   325\t* :meth:`.QuerySet.bulk_update` now returns the number of objects updated.\n   326\t\n   327\t* The new :attr:`.Aggregate.empty_aggregate_value` attribute allows specifying\n   328\t  a value to return when the aggregation is used over an empty result set.\n   329\t\n   330\t* The ``skip_locked`` argument of :meth:`.QuerySet.select_for_update()` is now\n   331\t  allowed on MariaDB 10.6+.\n   332\t\n   333\t* :class:`~django.db.models.Lookup` expressions may now be used in ``QuerySet``\n   334\t  annotations, aggregations, and directly in filters.\n   335\t\n   336\t* The new :ref:`default <aggregate-default>` argument for built-in aggregates\n   337\t  allows specifying a value to be returned when the queryset (or grouping)\n   338\t  contains no entries, rather than ``None``.\n   339\t\n   340\tRequests and Responses\n   341\t~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~\n   342\t\n   343\t* The :class:`~django.middleware.security.SecurityMiddleware` now adds the\n   344\t  :ref:`Cross-Origin Opener Policy <cross-origin-opener-policy>` header with a\n   345\t  value of ``'same-origin'`` to prevent cross-origin popups from sharing the\n   346\t  same browsing context. You can prevent this header from being added by\n   347\t  setting the :setting:`SECURE_CROSS_ORIGIN_OPENER_POLICY` setting to ``None``.\n   348\t\n   349\tSecurity\n   350\t~~~~~~~~\n   351\t\n   352\t* ...\n   353\t\n   354\tSerialization\n   355\t~~~~~~~~~~~~~\n   356\t\n   357\t* ...\n   358\t\n   359\tSignals\n   360\t~~~~~~~\n   361\t\n   362\t* The new ``stdout`` argument for :func:`~django.db.models.signals.pre_migrate`\n   363\t  and :func:`~django.db.models.signals.post_migrate` signals allows redirecting\n   364\t  output to a stream-like object. It should be preferred over\n   365\t  :py:data:`sys.stdout` and :py:func:`print` when emitting verbose output in\n   366\t  order to allow proper capture when testing.\n   367\t\n   368\tTemplates\n   369\t~~~~~~~~~\n   370\t\n   371\t* :tfilter:`floatformat` template filter now allows using the ``u`` suffix to\n   372\t  force disabling localization.\n   373\t\n   374\tTests\n   375\t~~~~~\n   376\t\n   377\t* The new ``serialized_aliases`` argument of\n   378\t  :func:`django.test.utils.setup_databases` determines which\n   379\t  :setting:`DATABASES` aliases test databases should have their state\n   380\t  serialized to allow usage of the\n   381\t  :ref:`serialized_rollback <test-case-serialized-rollback>` feature.\n   382\t\n   383\t* Django test runner now supports a :option:`--buffer <test --buffer>` option\n   384\t  with parallel tests.\n   385\t\n   386\t* The new ``logger`` argument to :class:`~django.test.runner.DiscoverRunner`\n   387\t  allows a Python :py:ref:`logger <logger>` to be used for logging.\n   388\t\n   389\t* The new :meth:`.DiscoverRunner.log` method provides a way to log messages\n   390\t  that uses the ``DiscoverRunner.logger``, or prints to the console if not set.\n   391\t\n   392\t* Django test runner now supports a :option:`--shuffle <test --shuffle>` option\n   393\t  to execute tests in a random order.\n   394\t\n   395\t* The :option:`test --parallel` option now supports the value ``auto`` to run\n   396\t  one test process for each processor core.\n   397\t\n   398\t* :meth:`.TestCase.captureOnCommitCallbacks` now captures new callbacks added\n   399\t  while executing :func:`.transaction.on_commit` callbacks.\n   400\t\n   401\tURLs\n   402\t~~~~\n   403\t\n   404\t* ...\n   405\t\n   406\tUtilities\n   407\t~~~~~~~~~\n   408\t\n   409\t* ...\n   410\t\n   411\tValidators\n   412\t~~~~~~~~~~\n   413\t\n   414\t* ...\n   415\t\n   416\t.. _backwards-incompatible-4.0:\n   417\t\n   418\tBackwards incompatible changes in 4.0\n   419\t=====================================\n   420\t\n   421\tDatabase backend API\n   422\t--------------------\n   423\t\n   424\tThis section describes changes that may be needed in third-party database\n   425\tbackends.\n   426\t\n   427\t* ``DatabaseOperations.year_lookup_bounds_for_date_field()`` and\n   428\t  ``year_lookup_bounds_for_datetime_field()`` methods now take the optional\n   429\t  ``iso_year`` argument in order to support bounds for ISO-8601 week-numbering\n   430\t  years.\n   431\t\n   432\t* The second argument of ``DatabaseSchemaEditor._unique_sql()`` and\n   433\t  ``_create_unique_sql()`` methods is now fields instead of columns.\n   434\t\n   435\t:mod:`django.contrib.gis`\n   436\t-------------------------\n   437\t\n   438\t* Support for PostGIS 2.3 is removed.\n   439\t\n   440\t* Support for GDAL 2.0 and GEOS 3.5 is removed.\n   441\t\n   442\tDropped support for PostgreSQL 9.6\n   443\t----------------------------------\n   444\t\n   445\tUpstream support for PostgreSQL 9.6 ends in November 2021. Django 4.0 supports\n   446\tPostgreSQL 10 and higher.\n   447\t\n   448\tDropped support for Oracle 12.2 and 18c\n   449\t---------------------------------------\n   450\t\n   451\tUpstream support for Oracle 12.2 ends in March 2022 and for Oracle 18c it ends\n   452\tin June 2021. Django 3.2 will be supported until April 2024. Django 4.0\n   453\tofficially supports Oracle 19c.\n   454\t\n   455\t.. _csrf-trusted-origins-changes-4.0:\n   456\t\n   457\t``CSRF_TRUSTED_ORIGINS`` changes\n   458\t--------------------------------\n   459\t\n   460\tFormat change\n   461\t~~~~~~~~~~~~~\n   462\t\n   463\tValues in the :setting:`CSRF_TRUSTED_ORIGINS` setting must include the scheme\n   464\t(e.g. ``'http://'`` or ``'https://'``) instead of only the hostname.\n   465\t\n   466\tAlso, values that started with a dot, must now also include an asterisk before\n   467\tthe dot. For example, change ``'.example.com'`` to ``'https://*.example.com'``.\n   468\t\n   469\tA system check detects any required changes.\n   470\t\n   471\tConfiguring it may now be required\n   472\t~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~\n   473\t\n   474\tAs CSRF protection now consults the ``Origin`` header, you may need to set\n   475\t:setting:`CSRF_TRUSTED_ORIGINS`, particularly if you allow requests from\n   476\tsubdomains by setting :setting:`CSRF_COOKIE_DOMAIN` (or\n   477\t:setting:`SESSION_COOKIE_DOMAIN` if :setting:`CSRF_USE_SESSIONS` is enabled) to\n   478\ta value starting with a dot.\n   479\t\n   480\t``SecurityMiddleware`` no longer sets the ``X-XSS-Protection`` header\n   481\t---------------------------------------------------------------------\n   482\t\n   483\tThe :class:`~django.middleware.security.SecurityMiddleware` no longer sets the\n   484\t``X-XSS-Protection`` header if the ``SECURE_BROWSER_XSS_FILTER`` setting is\n   485\t``True``. The setting is removed.\n   486\t\n   487\tMost modern browsers don't honor the ``X-XSS-Protection`` HTTP header. You can\n   488\tuse Content-Security-Policy_ without allowing ``'unsafe-inline'`` scripts\n   489\tinstead.\n   490\t\n   491\tIf you want to support legacy browsers and set the header, use this line in a\n   492\tcustom middleware::\n   493\t\n   494\t    response.headers.setdefault('X-XSS-Protection', '1; mode=block')\n   495\t\n   496\t.. _Content-Security-Policy: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Headers/Content-Security-Policy\n   497\t\n   498\tMigrations autodetector changes\n   499\t-------------------------------\n   500\t\n   501\tThe migrations autodetector now uses model states instead of model classes.\n   502\tAlso, migration operations for ``ForeignKey`` and ``ManyToManyField`` fields no\n   503\tlonger specify attributes which were not passed to the fields during\n   504\tinitialization.\n   505\t\n   506\tAs a side-effect, running ``makemigrations`` might generate no-op\n   507\t``AlterField`` operations for ``ManyToManyField`` and ``ForeignKey`` fields in\n   508\tsome cases.\n   509\t\n   510\tMiscellaneous\n   511\t-------------\n   512\t\n   513\t* Support for ``cx_Oracle`` < 7.0 is removed.\n   514\t\n   515\t* To allow serving a Django site on a subpath without changing the value of\n   516\t  :setting:`STATIC_URL`, the leading slash is removed from that setting (now\n   517\t  ``'static/'``) in the default :djadmin:`startproject` template.\n   518\t\n   519\t* The :class:`~django.contrib.admin.AdminSite` method for the admin ``index``\n   520\t  view is no longer decorated with ``never_cache`` when accessed directly,\n   521\t  rather than via the recommended ``AdminSite.urls`` property, or\n   522\t  ``AdminSite.get_urls()`` method.\n   523\t\n   524\t* Unsupported operations on a sliced queryset now raise ``TypeError`` instead\n   525\t  of ``AssertionError``.\n   526\t\n   527\t* The undocumented ``django.test.runner.reorder_suite()`` function is renamed\n   528\t  to ``reorder_tests()``. It now accepts an iterable of tests rather than a\n   529\t  test suite, and returns an iterator of tests.\n   530\t\n   531\t* Calling ``FileSystemStorage.delete()`` with an empty ``name`` now raises\n   532\t  ``ValueError`` instead of ``AssertionError``.\n   533\t\n   534\t* Calling ``EmailMultiAlternatives.attach_alternative()`` or\n   535\t  ``EmailMessage.attach()`` with an invalid ``content`` or ``mimetype``\n   536\t  arguments now raise ``ValueError`` instead of ``AssertionError``.\n   537\t\n   538\t* :meth:`~django.test.SimpleTestCase.assertHTMLEqual` no longer considers a\n   539\t  non-boolean attribute without a value equal to an attribute with the same\n   540\t  name and value.\n   541\t\n   542\t* Tests that fail to load, for example due to syntax errors, now always match\n   543\t  when using :option:`test --tag`.\n   544\t\n   545\t* The undocumented ``django.contrib.admin.utils.lookup_needs_distinct()``\n   546\t  function is renamed to ``lookup_spawns_duplicates()``.\n   547\t\n   548\t* The undocumented ``HttpRequest.get_raw_uri()`` method is removed. The\n   549\t  :meth:`.HttpRequest.build_absolute_uri` method may be a suitable alternative.\n   550\t\n   551\t* The ``object`` argument of undocumented ``ModelAdmin.log_addition()``,\n   552\t  ``log_change()``, and ``log_deletion()`` methods is renamed to ``obj``.\n   553\t\n   554\t* :class:`~django.utils.feedgenerator.RssFeed`,\n   555\t  :class:`~django.utils.feedgenerator.Atom1Feed`, and their subclasses now\n   556\t  emit elements with no content as self-closing tags.\n   557\t\n   558\t* ``NodeList.render()`` no longer casts the output of ``render()`` method for\n   559\t  individual nodes to a string. ``Node.render()`` should always return a string\n   560\t  as documented.\n   561\t\n   562\t* The ``where_class`` property of ``django.db.models.sql.query.Query`` and the\n   563\t  ``where_class`` argument to the private ``get_extra_restriction()`` method of\n   564\t  ``ForeignObject`` and ``ForeignObjectRel`` are removed. If needed, initialize\n   565\t  ``django.db.models.sql.where.WhereNode`` instead.\n   566\t\n   567\t* The ``filter_clause`` argument of the undocumented ``Query.add_filter()``\n   568\t  method is replaced by two positional arguments ``filter_lhs`` and\n   569\t  ``filter_rhs``.\n   570\t\n   571\t* :class:`~django.middleware.csrf.CsrfViewMiddleware` now uses\n   572\t  ``request.META['CSRF_COOKIE_NEEDS_UPDATE']`` in place of\n   573\t  ``request.META['CSRF_COOKIE_USED']``, ``request.csrf_cookie_needs_reset``,\n   574\t  and ``response.csrf_cookie_set`` to track whether the CSRF cookie should be\n   575\t  sent. This is an undocumented, private API.\n   576\t\n   577\t* The undocumented ``TRANSLATOR_COMMENT_MARK`` constant is moved from\n   578\t  ``django.template.base`` to ``django.utils.translation.template``.\n   579\t\n   580\t* The ``real_apps`` argument of the undocumented\n   581\t  ``django.db.migrations.state.ProjectState.__init__()`` method must now be a\n   582\t  set if provided.\n   583\t\n   584\t* :class:`~django.forms.RadioSelect` and\n   585\t  :class:`~django.forms.CheckboxSelectMultiple` widgets are now rendered in\n   586\t  ``<div>`` tags so they are announced more concisely by screen readers. If you\n   587\t  need the previous behavior, :ref:`override the widget template\n   588\t  <overriding-built-in-widget-templates>` with the appropriate template from\n   589\t  Django 3.2.\n   590\t\n   591\t* The :tfilter:`floatformat` template filter no longer depends on the\n   592\t  ``USE_L10N`` setting and always returns localized output. Use the ``u``\n   593\t  suffix to disable localization.\n   594\t\n   595\t* The default value of the ``USE_L10N`` setting is changed to ``True``. See the\n   596\t  :ref:`Localization section <use_l10n_deprecation>` above for more details.\n   597\t\n   598\t.. _deprecated-features-4.0:\n   599\t\n   600\tFeatures deprecated in 4.0\n   601\t==========================\n   602\t\n   603\tTime zone support\n   604\t-----------------\n   605\t\n   606\tIn order to follow good practice, the default value of the :setting:`USE_TZ`\n   607\tsetting will change from ``False`` to ``True``, and time zone support will be\n   608\tenabled by default, in Django 5.0.\n   609\t\n   610\tNote that the default :file:`settings.py` file created by\n   611\t:djadmin:`django-admin startproject <startproject>` includes\n   612\t:setting:`USE_TZ = True <USE_TZ>` since Django 1.4.\n   613\t\n   614\tYou can set ``USE_TZ`` to ``False`` in your project settings before then to\n   615\topt-out.\n   616\t\n   617\t.. _use_l10n_deprecation:\n   618\t\n   619\tLocalization\n   620\t------------\n   621\t\n   622\tIn order to follow good practice, the default value of the ``USE_L10N`` setting\n   623\tis changed from ``False`` to ``True``.\n   624\t\n   625\tMoreover ``USE_L10N`` is deprecated as of this release. Starting with Django\n   626\t5.0, by default, any date or number displayed by Django will be localized.\n   627\t\n   628\tThe :ttag:`{% localize %} <localize>` tag and the :tfilter:`localize`/\n   629\t:tfilter:`unlocalize` filters will still be honored by Django.\n   630\t\n   631\tMiscellaneous\n   632\t-------------\n   633\t\n   634\t* ``SERIALIZE`` test setting is deprecated as it can be inferred from the\n   635\t  :attr:`~django.test.TestCase.databases` with the\n   636\t  :ref:`serialized_rollback <test-case-serialized-rollback>` option enabled.\n   637\t\n   638\t* The undocumented ``django.utils.baseconv`` module is deprecated.\n   639\t\n   640\t* The undocumented ``django.utils.datetime_safe`` module is deprecated.\n   641\t\n   642\t* The default sitemap protocol for sitemaps built outside the context of a\n   643\t  request will change from ``'http'`` to ``'https'`` in Django 5.0.\n   644\t\n   645\t* The ``extra_tests`` argument for :meth:`.DiscoverRunner.build_suite` and\n   646\t  :meth:`.DiscoverRunner.run_tests` is deprecated.\n   647\t\n   648\t* The :class:`~django.contrib.postgres.aggregates.ArrayAgg`,\n   649\t  :class:`~django.contrib.postgres.aggregates.JSONBAgg`, and\n   650\t  :class:`~django.contrib.postgres.aggregates.StringAgg` aggregates will return\n   651\t  ``None`` when there are no rows instead of ``[]``, ``[]``, and ``''``\n   652\t  respectively in Django 5.0. If you need the previous behavior, explicitly set\n   653\t  ``default`` to ``Value([])``, ``Value('[]')``, or ``Value('')``.\n   654\t\n   655\tFeatures removed in 4.0\n   656\t=======================\n   657\t\n   658\tThese features have reached the end of their deprecation cycle and are removed\n   659\tin Django 4.0.\n   660\t\n   661\tSee :ref:`deprecated-features-3.0` for details on these changes, including how\n   662\tto remove usage of these features.\n   663\t\n   664\t* ``django.utils.http.urlquote()``, ``urlquote_plus()``, ``urlunquote()``, and\n   665\t  ``urlunquote_plus()`` are removed.\n   666\t\n   667\t* ``django.utils.encoding.force_text()`` and ``smart_text()`` are removed.\n   668\t\n   669\t* ``django.utils.translation.ugettext()``, ``ugettext_lazy()``,\n   670\t  ``ugettext_noop()``, ``ungettext()``, and ``ungettext_lazy()`` are removed.\n   671\t\n   672\t* ``django.views.i18n.set_language()`` doesn't set the user language in\n   673\t  ``request.session`` (key ``_language``).\n   674\t\n   675\t* ``alias=None`` is required in the signature of\n   676\t  ``django.db.models.Expression.get_group_by_cols()`` subclasses.\n   677\t\n   678\t* ``django.utils.text.unescape_entities()`` is removed.\n   679\t\n   680\t* ``django.utils.http.is_safe_url()`` is removed.\n   681\t\n   682\tSee :ref:`deprecated-features-3.1` for details on these changes, including how\n   683\tto remove usage of these features.\n   684\t\n   685\t* The ``PASSWORD_RESET_TIMEOUT_DAYS`` setting is removed.\n   686\t\n   687\t* The :lookup:`isnull` lookup no longer allows using non-boolean values as the\n   688\t  right-hand side.\n   689\t\n   690\t* The ``django.db.models.query_utils.InvalidQuery`` exception class is removed.\n   691\t\n   692\t* The ``django-admin.py`` entry point is removed.\n   693\t\n   694\t* The ``HttpRequest.is_ajax()`` method is removed.\n   695\t\n   696\t* Support for the pre-Django 3.1 encoding format of cookies values used by\n   697\t  ``django.contrib.messages.storage.cookie.CookieStorage`` is removed.\n   698\t\n   699\t* Support for the pre-Django 3.1 password reset tokens in the admin site (that\n   700\t  use the SHA-1 hashing algorithm) is removed.\n   701\t\n   702\t* Support for the pre-Django 3.1 encoding format of sessions is removed.\n   703\t\n   704\t* Support for the pre-Django 3.1 ``django.core.signing.Signer`` signatures\n   705\t  (encoded with the SHA-1 algorithm) is removed.\n   706\t\n   707\t* Support for the pre-Django 3.1 ``django.core.signing.dumps()`` signatures\n   708\t  (encoded with the SHA-1 algorithm) in ``django.core.signing.loads()`` is\n   709\t  removed.\n   710\t\n   711\t* Support for the pre-Django 3.1 user sessions (that use the SHA-1 algorithm)\n   712\t  is removed.\n   713\t\n   714\t* The ``get_response`` argument for\n   715\t  ``django.utils.deprecation.MiddlewareMixin.__init__()`` is required and\n   716\t  doesn't accept ``None``.\n   717\t\n   718\t* The ``providing_args`` argument for ``django.dispatch.Signal`` is removed.\n   719\t\n   720\t* The ``length`` argument for ``django.utils.crypto.get_random_string()`` is\n   721\t  required.\n   722\t\n   723\t* The ``list`` message for ``ModelMultipleChoiceField`` is removed.\n   724\t\n   725\t* Support for passing raw column aliases to ``QuerySet.order_by()`` is removed.\n   726\t\n   727\t* The ``NullBooleanField`` model field is removed, except for support in\n   728\t  historical migrations.\n   729\t\n   730\t* ``django.conf.urls.url()`` is removed.\n   731\t\n   732\t* The ``django.contrib.postgres.fields.JSONField`` model field is removed,\n   733\t  except for support in historical migrations.\n   734\t\n   735\t* ``django.contrib.postgres.fields.jsonb.KeyTransform`` and\n   736\t  ``django.contrib.postgres.fields.jsonb.KeyTextTransform`` are removed.\n   737\t\n   738\t* ``django.contrib.postgres.forms.JSONField`` is removed.\n   739\t\n   740\t* The ``{% ifequal %}`` and ``{% ifnotequal %}`` template tags are removed.\n   741\t\n the ``future``\n   461\t  template tag library (used during the 1.6/1.7 deprecation period).\n   462\t\n   463\t* ``django.conf.urls.patterns()`` will be removed.\n   464\t\n   465\t* Support for the ``prefix`` argument to\n   466\t  ``django.conf.urls.i18n.i18n_patterns()`` will be removed.\n   467\t\n   468\t* ``SimpleTestCase.urls`` will be removed.\n   469\t\n   470\t* Using an incorrect count of unpacked values in the ``for`` template tag\n   471\t  will raise an exception rather than fail silently.\n   472\t\n   473\t* The ability to reverse URLs using a dotted Python path will be removed.\n   474\t\n   475\t* The ability to use a dotted Python path for the ``LOGIN_URL`` and\n   476\t  ``LOGIN_REDIRECT_URL`` settings will be removed.\n   477\t\n   478\t* Support for :py:mod:`optparse` will be dropped for custom management commands\n   479\t  (replaced by :py:mod:`argparse`).\n   480\t\n   481\t* The class ``django.core.management.NoArgsCommand`` will be removed. Use\n   482\t  :class:`~django.core.management.BaseCommand` instead, which takes no arguments\n   483\t  by default.\n   484\t\n   485\t* ``django.core.context_processors`` module will be removed.\n   486\t\n   487\t* ``django.db.models.sql.aggregates`` module will be removed.\n   488\t\n   489\t* ``django.contrib.gis.db.models.sql.aggregates`` module will be removed.\n   490\t\n   491\t* The following methods and properties of ``django.db.sql.query.Query`` will\n   492\t  be removed:\n   493\t\n   494\t  * Properties: ``aggregates`` and ``aggregate_select``\n   495\t  * Methods: ``add_aggregate``, ``set_aggregate_mask``, and\n   496\t    ``append_aggregate_mask``.\n   497\t\n   498\t* ``django.template.resolve_variable`` will be removed.\n   499\t\n   500\t* The following private APIs will be removed from\n   501\t  :class:`django.db.models.options.Options` (``Model._meta``):\n   502\t\n   503\t  * ``get_field_by_name()``\n   504\t  * ``get_all_field_names()``\n   505\t  * ``get_fields_with_model()``\n   506\t  * ``get_concrete_fields_with_model()``\n   507\t  * ``get_m2m_with_model()``\n   508\t  * ``get_all_related_objects()``\n   509\t  * ``get_all_related_objects_with_model()``\n   510\t  * ``get_all_related_many_to_many_objects()``\n   511\t  * ``get_all_related_m2m_objects_with_model()``\n   512\t\n   513\t* The ``error_message`` argument of ``django.forms.RegexField`` will be removed.\n   514\t\n   515\t* The ``unordered_list`` filter will no longer support old style lists.\n   516\t\n   517\t* Support for string ``view`` arguments to ``url()`` will be removed.\n   518\t\n   519\t* The backward compatible shim  to rename ``django.forms.Form._has_changed()``\n   520\t  to ``has_changed()`` will be removed.\n   521\t\n   522\t* The ``removetags`` template filter will be removed.\n   523\t\n   524\t* The ``remove_tags()`` and ``strip_entities()`` functions in\n   525\t  ``django.utils.html`` will be removed.\n   526\t\n   527\t* The ``is_admin_site`` argument to\n   528\t  ``django.contrib.auth.views.password_reset()`` will be removed.\n   529\t\n   530\t* ``django.db.models.field.subclassing.SubfieldBase`` will be removed.\n   531\t\n   532\t* ``django.utils.checksums`` will be removed; its functionality is included\n   533\t  in ``django-localflavor`` 1.1+.\n   534\t\n   535\t* The ``original_content_type_id`` attribute on\n   536\t  ``django.contrib.admin.helpers.InlineAdminForm`` will be removed.\n   537\t\n   538\t* The backwards compatibility shim to allow ``FormMixin.get_form()`` to be\n   539\t  defined with no default value for its ``form_class`` argument will be removed.\n   540\t\n   541\t* The following settings will be removed:\n   542\t\n   543\t  * ``ALLOWED_INCLUDE_ROOTS``\n   544\t  * ``TEMPLATE_CONTEXT_PROCESSORS``\n   545\t  * ``TEMPLATE_DEBUG``\n   546\t  * ``TEMPLATE_DIRS``\n   547\t  * ``TEMPLATE_LOADERS``\n   548\t  * ``TEMPLATE_STRING_IF_INVALID``\n   549\t\n   550\t* The backwards compatibility alias ``django.template.loader.BaseLoader`` will\n   551\t  be removed.\n   552\t\n   553\t* Django template objects returned by\n   554\t  :func:`~django.template.loader.get_template` and\n   555\t  :func:`~django.template.loader.select_template` won't accept a\n   556\t  :class:`~django.template.Context` in their\n   557\t  :meth:`~django.template.backends.base.Template.render()` method anymore.\n   558\t\n   559\t* :doc:`Template response APIs </ref/template-response>` will enforce the use\n   560\t  of :class:`dict` and backend-dependent template objects instead of\n   561\t  :class:`~django.template.Context` and :class:`~django.template.Template`\n   562\t  respectively.\n   563\t\n   564\t* The ``current_app`` parameter for the following function and classes will be\n   565\t  removed:\n   566\t\n   567\t  * ``django.shortcuts.render()``\n   568\t  * ``django.template.Context()``\n   569\t  * ``django.template.RequestContext()``\n   570\t  * ``django.template.response.TemplateResponse()``\n   571\t\n   572\t* The ``dictionary`` and ``context_instance`` parameters for the following\n   573\t  functions will be removed:\n   574\t\n   575\t  * ``django.shortcuts.render()``\n   576\t  * ``django.shortcuts.render_to_response()``\n   577\t  * ``django.template.loader.render_to_string()``\n   578\t\n   579\t* The ``dirs`` parameter for the following functions will be removed:\n   580\t\n   581\t  * ``django.template.loader.get_template()``\n   582\t  * ``django.template.loader.select_template()``\n   583\t  * ``django.shortcuts.render()``\n   584\t  * ``django.shortcuts.render_to_response()``\n   585\t\n   586\t* Session verification will be enabled regardless of whether or not\n   587\t  ``'django.contrib.auth.middleware.SessionAuthenticationMiddleware'`` is in\n   588\t  ``MIDDLEWARE_CLASSES``.\n   589\t\n   590\t* Private attribute ``django.db.models.Field.related`` will be removed.\n   591\t\n   592\t* The ``--list`` option of the ``migrate`` management command will be removed.\n   593\t\n   594\t* The ``ssi`` template tag will be removed.\n   595\t\n   596\t* Support for the ``=`` comparison operator in the ``if`` template tag will be\n   597\t  removed.\n   598\t\n   599\t* The backwards compatibility shims to allow ``Storage.get_available_name()``\n   600\t  and ``Storage.save()`` to be defined without a ``max_length`` argument will\n   601\t  be removed.\n   602\t\n   603\t* Support for the legacy ``%(<foo>)s`` syntax in ``ModelFormMixin.success_url``\n   604\t  will be removed.\n   605\t\n   606\t* ``GeoQuerySet`` aggregate methods ``collect()``, ``extent()``, ``extent3d()``,\n   607\t  ``make_line()``, and ``unionagg()`` will be removed.\n   608\t\n   609\t* Ability to specify ``ContentType.name`` when creating a content type instance\n   610\t  will be removed.\n   611\t\n   612\t* Support for the old signature of ``allow_migrate`` will be removed. It changed\n   613\t  from ``allow_migrate(self, db, model)`` to\n   614\t  ``allow_migrate(self, db, app_label, model_name=None, **hints)``.\n   615\t\n   616\t* Support for the syntax of ``{% cycle %}`` that uses comma-separated arguments\n   617\t  will be removed.\n   618\t\n   619\t* The warning that :class:`~django.core.signing.Signer` issues when given an\n   620\t  invalid separator will become an exception.\n   621\t\n   622\t.. _deprecation-removed-in-1.9:\n   623\t\n   624\t1.9\n   625\t---\n   626\t\n   627\tSee the :ref:`Django 1.7 release notes<deprecated-features-1.7>` for more\n   628\tdetails on these changes.\n   629\t\n   630\t* ``django.utils.dictconfig`` will be removed.\n   631\t\n   632\t* ``django.utils.importlib`` will be removed.\n   633\t\n   634\t* ``django.utils.tzinfo`` will be removed.\n   635\t\n   636\t* ``django.utils.unittest`` will be removed.\n   637\t\n   638\t* The ``syncdb`` command will be removed.\n   639\t\n   640\t* ``django.db.models.signals.pre_syncdb`` and\n   641\t  ``django.db.models.signals.post_syncdb`` will be removed.\n   642\t\n   643\t* ``allow_syncdb`` on database routers will no longer automatically become\n   644\t  ``allow_migrate``.\n   645\t\n   646\t* Automatic syncing of apps without migrations will be removed. Migrations will\n   647\t  become compulsory for all apps unless you pass the ``--run-syncdb`` option to\n   648\t  ``migrate``.\n   649\t\n   650\t* The SQL management commands for apps without migrations, ``sql``, ``sqlall``,\n   651\t  ``sqlclear``, ``sqldropindexes``, and ``sqlindexes``, will be removed.\n   652\t\n   653\t* Support for automatic loading of ``initial_data`` fixtures and initial SQL\n   654\t  data will be removed.\n   655\t\n   656\t* All models will need to be defined inside an installed application or\n   657\t  declare an explicit :attr:`~django.db.models.Options.app_label`.\n   658\t  Furthermore, it won't be possible to import them before their application\n   659\t  is loaded. In particular, it won't be possible to import models inside\n   660\t  the root package of their application.\n   661\t\n   662\t* The model and form ``IPAddressField`` will be removed. A stub field will\n   663\t  remain for compatibility with historical migrations.\n   664\t\n   665\t* ``AppCommand.handle_app()`` will no longer be supported.\n   666\t\n   667\t* ``RequestSite`` and ``get_current_site()`` will no longer be importable from\n   668\t  ``django.contrib.sites.models``.\n   669\t\n   670\t* FastCGI support via the ``runfcgi`` management command will be\n   671\t  removed. Please deploy your project using WSGI.\n   672\t\n   673\t* ``django.utils.datastructures.SortedDict`` will be removed. Use\n   674\t  :class:`collections.OrderedDict` from the Python standard library instead.\n   675\t\n   676\t* ``ModelAdmin.declared_fieldsets`` will be removed.\n   677\t\n   678\t* Instances of ``util.py`` in the Django codebase have been renamed to\n   679\t  ``utils.py`` in an effort to unify all util and utils references.\n   680\t  The modules that provided backwards compatibility will be removed:\n   681\t\n   682\t  * ``django.contrib.admin.util``\n   683\t  * ``django.contrib.gis.db.backends.util``\n   684\t  * ``django.db.backends.util``\n   685\t  * ``django.forms.util``\n   686\t\n   687\t* ``ModelAdmin.get_formsets`` will be removed.\n   688\t\n   689\t* The backward compatibility shim introduced to rename the\n   690\t  ``BaseMemcachedCache._get_memcache_timeout()`` method to\n   691\t  ``get_backend_timeout()`` will be removed.\n   692\t\n   693\t* The ``--natural`` and ``-n`` options for :djadmin:`dumpdata` will be removed.\n   694\t\n   695\t* The ``use_natural_keys`` argument for ``serializers.serialize()`` will be\n   696\t  removed.\n   697\t\n   698\t* Private API ``django.forms.forms.get_declared_fields()`` will be removed.\n   699\t\n   700\t* The ability to use a ``SplitDateTimeWidget`` with ``DateTimeField`` will be\n   701\t  removed.\n   702\t\n   703\t* The ``WSGIRequest.REQUEST`` property will be removed.\n   704\t\n   705\t* The class ``django.utils.datastructures.MergeDict`` will be removed.\n   706\t\n   707\t* The ``zh-cn`` and ``zh-tw`` language codes will be removed and have been\n   708\t  replaced by the ``zh-hans`` and ``zh-hant`` language code respectively.\n   709\t\n   710\t* The internal ``django.utils.functional.memoize`` will be removed.\n   711\t\n   712\t* ``django.core.cache.get_cache`` will be removed. Add suitable entries\n   713\t  to :setting:`CACHES` and use :data:`django.core.cache.caches` instead.\n   714\t\n   715\t* ``django.db.models.loading`` will be removed.\n   716\t\n   717\t* Passing callable arguments to querysets will no longer be possible.\n   718\t\n   719\t* ``BaseCommand.requires_model_validation`` will be removed in favor of\n   720\t  ``requires_system_checks``. Admin validators will be replaced by admin\n   721\t  checks.\n   722\t\n   723\t* The ``ModelAdmin.validator_class`` and ``default_validator_class`` attributes\n   724\t  will be removed.\n   725\t\n   726\t* ``ModelAdmin.validate()`` will be removed.\n   727\t\n   728\t* ``django.db.backends.DatabaseValidation.validate_field`` will be removed in\n   729\t  favor of the ``check_field`` method.\n   730\t\n   731\t* The ``validate`` management command will be removed.\n   732\t\n   733\t* ``django.utils.module_loading.import_by_path`` will be removed in favor of\n   734\t  ``django.utils.module_loading.import_string``.\n   735\t\n   736\t* ``ssi`` and ``url`` template tags will be removed from the ``future`` template\n   737\t  tag library (used during the 1.3/1.4 deprecation period).\n   738\t\n   739\t* ``django.utils.text.javascript_quote`` will be removed.\n   740\t\n   741\t* Database test settings as independent entries in the database settings,\n   742\t  prefixed by ``TEST_``, will no longer be supported.\n   743\t\n   744\t* The ``cache_choices`` option to :class:`~django.forms.ModelChoiceField` and\n   745\t  :class:`~django.forms.ModelMultipleChoiceField` will be removed.\n   746\t\n   747\t* The default value of the\n   748\t  :attr:`RedirectView.permanent <django.views.generic.base.RedirectView.permanent>`\n   749\t  attribute will change from ``True`` to ``False``.\n   750\t\n   751\t* ``django.contrib.sitemaps.FlatPageSitemap`` will be removed in favor of\n   752\t  ``django.contrib.flatpages.sitemaps.FlatPageSitemap``.\n   753\t\n   754\t* Private API ``django.test.utils.TestTemplateLoader`` will be removed.\n   755\t\n   756\t* The ``django.contrib.contenttypes.generic`` module will be removed.\n   757\t\n   758\t* Private APIs ``django.db.models.sql.where.WhereNode.make_atom()`` and\n   759\t  ``django.db.models.sql.where.Constraint`` will be removed.\n   760\t\n   761\t.. _deprecation-removed-in-1.8:\n   762\t\n   763\t1.8\n   764\t---\n   765\t\n   766\tSee the :ref:`Django 1.6 release notes<deprecated-features-1.6>` for more\n   767\tdetails on these changes.\n   768\t\n   769\t* ``django.contrib.comments`` will be removed.\n   770\t\n   771\t* The following transaction management APIs will be removed:\n   772\t\n   773\t  - ``TransactionMiddleware``,\n   774\t  - the decorators and context managers ``autocommit``, ``commit_on_success``,\n   775\t    and ``commit_manually``, defined in ``django.db.transaction``,\n   776\t  - the functions ``commit_unless_managed`` and ``rollback_unless_managed``,\n   777\t    also defined in ``django.db.transaction``,\n   778\t  - the ``TRANSACTIONS_MANAGED`` setting.\n   779\t\n   780\t* The :ttag:`cycle` and :ttag:`firstof` template tags will auto-escape their\n   781\t  arguments. In 1.6 and 1.7, this behavior is provided by the version of these\n   782\t  tags in the ``future`` template tag library.\n   783\t\n   784\t* The ``SEND_BROKEN_LINK_EMAILS`` setting will be removed. Add the\n   785\t  :class:`django.middleware.common.BrokenLinkEmailsMiddleware` middleware to\n   786\t  your ``MIDDLEWARE_CLASSES`` setting instead.\n   787\t\n   788\t* ``django.middleware.doc.XViewMiddleware`` will be removed. Use\n   789\t  ``django.contrib.admindocs.middleware.XViewMiddleware`` instead.\n   790\t\n   791\t* ``Model._meta.module_name`` was renamed to ``model_name``.\n   792\t\n   793\t* Remove the backward compatible shims introduced to rename ``get_query_set``\n   794\t  and similar queryset methods. This affects the following classes:\n   795\t  ``BaseModelAdmin``, ``ChangeList``, ``BaseCommentNode``,\n   796\t  ``GenericForeignKey``, ``Manager``, ``SingleRelatedObjectDescriptor`` and\n   797\t  ``ReverseSingleRelatedObjectDescriptor``.\n   798\t\n   799\t* Remove the backward compatible shims introduced to rename the attributes\n   800\t  ``ChangeList.root_query_set`` and ``ChangeList.query_set``.\n   801\t\n   802\t* ``django.views.defaults.shortcut`` will be removed, as part of the\n   803\t  goal of removing all ``django.contrib`` references from the core\n   804\t  Django codebase. Instead use\n   805\t  ``django.contrib.contenttypes.views.shortcut``. ``django.conf.urls.shortcut``\n   806\t  will also be removed.\n   807\t\n   808\t* Support for the Python Imaging Library (PIL) module will be removed, as it\n   809\t  no longer appears to be actively maintained & does not work on Python 3.\n   810\t\n   811\t* The following private APIs will be removed:\n   812\t\n   813\t  - ``django.db.backend``\n   814\t  - ``django.db.close_connection()``\n   815\t  - ``django.db.backends.creation.BaseDatabaseCreation.set_autocommit()``\n   816\t  - ``django.db.transaction.is_managed()``\n   817\t  - ``django.db.transaction.managed()``\n   818\t\n   819\t* ``django.forms.widgets.RadioInput`` will be removed in favor of\n   820\t  ``django.forms.widgets.RadioChoiceInput``.\n   821\t\n   822\t* The module ``django.test.simple`` and the class\n   823\t  ``django.test.simple.DjangoTestSuiteRunner`` will be removed. Instead use\n   824\t  ``django.test.runner.DiscoverRunner``.\n   825\t\n   826\t* The module ``django.test._doctest`` will be removed. Instead use the doctest\n   827\t  module from the Python standard library.\n   828\t\n   829\t* The ``CACHE_MIDDLEWARE_ANONYMOUS_ONLY`` setting will be removed.\n   830\t\n   831\t* Usage of the hard-coded *Hold down \"Control\", or \"Command\" on a Mac, to select\n   832\t  more than one.* string to override or append to user-provided ``help_text`` in\n   833\t  forms for ManyToMany model fields will not be performed by Django anymore\n   834\t  either at the model or forms layer.\n   835\t\n   836\t* The ``Model._meta.get_(add|change|delete)_permission`` methods will\n   837\t  be removed.\n   838\t\n   839\t* The session key ``django_language`` will no longer be read for backwards\n   840\t  compatibility.\n   841\t\n   842\t* Geographic Sitemaps will be removed\n   843\t  (``django.contrib.gis.sitemaps.views.index`` and\n   844\t  ``django.contrib.gis.sitemaps.views.sitemap``).\n   845\t\n   846\t* ``django.utils.html.fix_ampersands``, the ``fix_ampersands`` template filter and\n   847\t  ``django.utils.html.clean_html`` will be removed following an accelerated deprecation.\n   848\t\n   849\t.. _deprecation-removed-in-1.7:\n   850\t\n   851\t1.7\n   852\t---\n   853\t\n   854\tSee the :ref:`Django 1.5 release notes<deprecated-features-1.5>` for more\n   855\tdetails on these changes.\n   856\t\n   857\t* The module ``django.utils.simplejson`` will be removed. The standard library\n   858\t  provides :mod:`json` which should be used instead.\n   859\t\n   860\t* The function ``django.utils.itercompat.product`` will be removed. The Python\n   861\t  builtin version should be used instead.\n   862\t\n   863\t* Auto-correction of INSTALLED_APPS and TEMPLATE_DIRS settings when they are\n   864\t  specified as a plain string instead of a tuple will be removed and raise an\n   865\t  exception.\n   866\t\n   867\t* The ``mimetype`` argument to the ``__init__`` methods of\n   868\t  :class:`~django.http.HttpResponse`,\n   869\t  :class:`~django.template.response.SimpleTemplateResponse`, and\n   870\t  :class:`~django.template.response.TemplateResponse`, will be removed.\n   871\t  ``content_type`` should be used instead. This also applies to the\n   872\t  ``render_to_response()`` shortcut and the sitemap views,\n   873\t  :func:`~django.contrib.sitemaps.views.index` and\n   874\t  :func:`~django.contrib.sitemaps.views.sitemap`.\n   875\t\n   876\t* When :class:`~django.http.HttpResponse` is instantiated with an iterator,\n   877\t  or when :attr:`~django.http.HttpResponse.content` is set to an iterator,\n   878\t  that iterator will be immediately consumed.\n   879\t\n   880\t* The ``AUTH_PROFILE_MODULE`` setting, and the ``get_profile()`` method on\n   881\t  the User model, will be removed.\n   882\t\n   883\t* The ``cleanup`` management command will be removed. It's replaced by\n   884\t  ``clearsessions``.\n   885\t\n   886\t* The ``daily_cleanup.py`` script will be removed.\n   887\t\n   888\t* The ``depth`` keyword argument will be removed from\n   889\t  :meth:`~django.db.models.query.QuerySet.select_related`.\n   890\t\n   891\t* The undocumented ``get_warnings_state()``/``restore_warnings_state()``\n   892\t  functions from :mod:`django.test.utils` and the ``save_warnings_state()``/\n   893\t  ``restore_warnings_state()``\n   894\t  :ref:`django.test.*TestCase <django-testcase-subclasses>` methods are\n   895\t  deprecated. Use the :class:`warnings.catch_warnings` context manager\n   896\t  available starting with Python 2.6 instead.\n   897\t\n   898\t* The undocumented ``check_for_test_cookie`` method in\n   899\t  :class:`~django.contrib.auth.forms.AuthenticationForm` will be removed\n   900\t  following an accelerated deprecation. Users subclassing this form should\n   901\t  remove calls to this method, and instead ensure that their auth related views\n   902\t  are CSRF protected, which ensures that cookies are enabled.\n   903\t\n   904\t* The version of ``django.contrib.auth.views.password_reset_confirm()`` that\n   905\t  supports base36 encoded user IDs\n   906\t  (``django.contrib.auth.views.password_reset_confirm_uidb36``) will be\n   907\t  removed. If your site has been running Django 1.6 for more than\n   908\t  ``PASSWORD_RESET_TIMEOUT_DAYS``, this change will have no effect. If not,\n   909\t  then any password reset links generated before you upgrade to Django 1.7\n   910\t  won't work after the upgrade.\n   911\t\n   912\t* The ``django.utils.encoding.StrAndUnicode`` mix-in will be removed.\n   913\t\n   914\t.. _deprecation-removed-in-1.6:\n   915\t\n   916\t1.6\n   917\t---\n   918\t\n   919\tSee the :ref:`Django 1.4 release notes<deprecated-features-1.4>` for more\n   920\tdetails on these changes.\n   921\t\n   922\t* ``django.contrib.databrowse`` will be removed.\n   923\t\n   924\t* ``django.contrib.localflavor`` will be removed following an accelerated\n   925\t  deprecation.\n   926\t\n   927\t* ``django.contrib.markup`` will be removed following an accelerated\n   928\t  deprecation.\n   929\t\n   930\t* The compatibility modules ``django.utils.copycompat`` and\n   931\t  ``django.utils.hashcompat`` as well as the functions\n   932\t  ``django.utils.itercompat.all`` and ``django.utils.itercompat.any`` will\n   933\t  be removed. The Python builtin versions should be used instead.\n   934\t\n   935\t* The ``csrf_response_exempt`` and ``csrf_view_exempt`` decorators will\n   936\t  be removed. Since 1.4 ``csrf_response_exempt`` has been a no-op (it\n   937\t  returns the same function), and ``csrf_view_exempt`` has been a\n   938\t  synonym for ``django.views.decorators.csrf.csrf_exempt``, which should\n   939\t  be used to replace it.\n   940\t\n   941\t* The ``django.core.cache.backends.memcached.CacheClass`` backend\n   942\t  was split into two in Django 1.3 in order to introduce support for\n   943\t  PyLibMC. The historical ``CacheClass`` will be removed in favor of\n   944\t  ``django.core.cache.backends.memcached.MemcachedCache``.\n   945\t\n   946\t* The UK-prefixed objects of ``django.contrib.localflavor.uk`` will only\n   947\t  be accessible through their GB-prefixed names (GB is the correct\n   948\t  ISO 3166 code for United Kingdom).\n   949\t\n   950\t* The ``IGNORABLE_404_STARTS`` and ``IGNORABLE_404_ENDS`` settings have been\n   951\t  superseded by :setting:`IGNORABLE_404_URLS` in the 1.4 release. They will be\n   952\t  removed.\n   953\t\n   954\t* The form wizard has been refactored to use class-based views with pluggable\n   955\t  backends in 1.4. The previous implementation will be removed.\n   956\t\n   957\t* Legacy ways of calling\n   958\t  :func:`~django.views.decorators.cache.cache_page` will be removed.\n   959\t\n   960\t* The backward-compatibility shim to automatically add a debug-false\n   961\t  filter to the ``'mail_admins'`` logging handler will be removed. The\n   962\t  :setting:`LOGGING` setting should include this filter explicitly if\n   963\t  it is desired.\n   964\t\n   965\t* The builtin truncation functions ``django.utils.text.truncate_words()``\n   966\t  and ``django.utils.text.truncate_html_words()`` will be removed in\n   967\t  favor of the ``django.utils.text.Truncator`` class.\n   968\t\n   969\t* The ``django.contrib.gis.geoip.GeoIP`` class was moved to\n   970\t  ``django.contrib.gis.geoip`` in 1.4 -- the shortcut in\n   971\t  ``django.contrib.gis.utils`` will be removed.\n   972\t\n   973\t* ``django.conf.urls.defaults`` will be removed. The functions\n   974\t  ``include()``, ``patterns()``, and ``url()``, plus\n   975\t  :data:`~django.conf.urls.handler404` and :data:`~django.conf.urls.handler500`\n   976\t  are now available through ``django.conf.urls``.\n   977\t\n   978\t* The functions ``setup_environ()`` and ``execute_manager()`` will be removed\n   979\t  from :mod:`django.core.management`. This also means that the old (pre-1.4)\n   980\t  style of :file:`manage.py` file will no longer work.\n   981\t\n   982\t* Setting the ``is_safe`` and ``needs_autoescape`` flags as attributes of\n   983\t  template filter functions will no longer be supported.\n   984\t\n   985\t* The attribute ``HttpRequest.raw_post_data`` was renamed to ``HttpRequest.body``\n   986\t  in 1.4. The backward compatibility will be removed --\n   987\t  ``HttpRequest.raw_post_data`` will no longer work.\n   988\t\n   989\t* The value for the ``post_url_continue`` parameter in\n   990\t  ``ModelAdmin.response_add()`` will have to be either ``None`` (to redirect\n   991\t  to the newly created object's edit page) or a pre-formatted url. String\n   992\t  formats, such as the previous default ``'../%s/'``, will not be accepted any\n   993\t  more.\n   994\t\n   995\t.. _deprecation-removed-in-1.5:\n   996\t\n   997\t1.5\n   998\t---\n   999\t\n  1000\tSee the :ref:`Django 1.3 release notes<deprecated-features-1.3>` for more\n","message":"1000 lines read from file starting from line 1.
